headline: 'Trump's anti-NATO outbursts have undeniably weakened the Atlantic pact'
description: By disparaging the North Atlantic Treaty Organization, Trump perhaps aims to assign blame for the United States' setbacks in the Gulf on Europe, columnist Alain Frachon wonders.

headline: First non-Iranian oil tanker crosses Strait of Hormuz since ceasefire
'Such carnage defies belief': Lebanon, crushed by Israeli bombs, counts its dead
Vladimir Putin announces Orthodox Easter ceasefire in Ukraine war
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky had proposed a temporary truce earlier this week.

headline: Macron to meet Pope Leo XIV on Friday to discuss a world in crisis
description: The French president and the American pontiff are expected to address conflicts in Lebanon and Iran and contemporary issues such as the dangers posed by the development of artificial intelligence.

headline: World Press Photo 2026: Abdulmonam Eassa honored for his coverage of the war in Sudan for Le Monde
description: French-Syrian photojournalist Abdulmonam Eassa received the prestigious World Press Photo award on Thursday in the 'Stories' category for his photo report 'War in Sudan, A Nation Trapped,' carried out in October 2024 and December 2025 for Le Monde.

headline: Denis Charbit: 'The Israeli lawmakers who established the death penalty merely obeyed their own instincts for vengeance'
description: The professor at the Open University of Israel looks into the recent law introducing the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of terrorist acts and anti-Israeli attacks. He sees it as a grave moral regression and is concerned about Israel abandoning 'thousands of years of ethics.'

Trump's escalatory rhetoric raises questions over his mental health
description: Members of Congress, stunned by the crude and genocidal language used by the president, have begun to discuss the US Constitution's 25th Amendment, which allows for the president to be declared unfit and for executive powers to be transferred to the vice president.

headline: Are Trump's ideologues intellectuals or imposters?
description: Curtis Yarvin has become a leading figure in the Neo-Reactionary movement (also known as the Dark Enlightenment), which has now made it to the White House. But can this blogger really be considered an intellectual? Political theorist Arnaud Miranda and sociologist Olivier Alexandre debate the origins and roles of one of the thinkers who has Trump's ear.

headline: In catacombs, museums, and labs, the delicate heritage of human remains
description: The Paris Catacombs reopen to the public on Wednesday, April 8, after five months of renovations. The timing offers a chance to reflect on how human remains are handled in cultural and research institutions. These singular relics pose ethical, technical and legal questions.

headline: War in Iran: Trump's elusive victory
